visual_elements.description: Projectile motion is the curved path an object follows when thrown or propelled through space. It occurs under the influence of gravity and no other force.

To analyze projectile motion, we consider two independent components: horizontal (x-axis) velocity remains constant as there's negligible air resistance; vertical (y-axis) acceleration due to gravity at 9.8 m/s² downward affecting the object throughout its trajectory.
The initial speed and angle of projection determine the range, maximum height, and flight time using kinematic equations which take into account both horizontal and vertical motions.
